page: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is an EDSv5-family virtual machine built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The memory configuration provides 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Local storage consists of a 2458 GB disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage. The system operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with the passmark CPU score and stress-ng multi-core CPU tests placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also strong, measuring 292.62 GB/sec for read and 134.23 GB/sec for write operations. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with clang, and database operations. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average category.

While the server excels in multi-threaded workloads and memory-intensive operations, it exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. Specifically, LLM inference text generation speed for a large 70B model is weak, registering at 2.72 tokens/sec. Given the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong database benchmark results, this server is configured for memory-optimized workloads, database operations, software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving, rather than large-scale AI inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is an EDSv5-family virtual machine configured with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, delivering a memory-optimized 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 physical cores, the server includes a 2458 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. Multi-core benchmarks show strong performance, particularly in database operations, software compilation, and large-block memory bandwidth. However, single-core processing and large-model LLM inference represent relative performance bottlenecks, with text generation for a 70B model falling into the bottom 25%. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive enterprise applications, database hosting, and multi-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, designed for database operations and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 2458 GB local disk, running on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four virtual CPUs, half a terabyte of RAM, and local storage for database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB RAM providing a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz
2458 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU and database operation benchmark results
Weak performance on large-model (70B) LLM text generation
Best suited for database hosting, software compilation, and memory-intensive tasks
